user_input:
summurize this please 
Here is the UI-09A wrap-up.

Diagnosis (which items were true)
#	Assessment
1
Can still be true if the taskbar pin was made from an older .lnk (PowerShell target) or from Launch-Agent-UI.cmd. Pinned shortcuts keep the old target until you unpin and pin a new shortcut.
2
Yes — this was the main design bug for the repo shortcut. Mimi AI Agent UI.lnk used powershell.exe -WindowStyle Hidden -File Launch-Agent-UI-Silent.ps1. PowerShell is a console-subsystem host; even when hidden it can show up as a terminal-style window/process in previews.
3
Secondary: the child used python.exe (also console subsystem). CreateNoWindow helps but the parent chain was the bigger issue.
4
Possible if the pin was never the repo script’s shortcut (e.g. pinned .cmd, Explorer “manual shortcut”, or another repo clone).
Root cause in one line: the normal demo shortcut went through powershell.exe → script → python.exe, so Windows still had a console-backed process in the chain; plus any stale pin would keep that behavior.

Fix (launcher level only)
Create-Agent-UI-Shortcut.ps1 now builds Mimi AI Agent UI.lnk with target = .venv-win\Scripts\pythonw.exe, arguments = -m streamlit run "<repo>\app\ui.py", working directory = repo root — no PowerShell, no cmd on the demo path.
Launch-Agent-UI-Silent.ps1 now prefers pythonw.exe when it exists (still useful for automation); Launch-Agent-UI.cmd stays the debug path (visible console, logs, Ctrl+C).
Docs: SYSTEM_EVAL_RUNBOOK.md, README.md, UX_log_system.md (UI-09A row), Launch-Agent-UI.cmd REM lines.
No changes to app/ui.py, playground.py, memory, or routing.

Regression: not run (launcher/docs only); say so in deliverables.

Deliverables
Root cause: Demo .lnk ran hidden PowerShell + python.exe, so a console-hosted process could still appear in thumbnails/previews; stale pins could lock in that path.
Files changed: Create-Agent-UI-Shortcut.ps1, Launch-Agent-UI-Silent.ps1, Launch-Agent-UI.cmd, docs/runbooks/SYSTEM_EVAL_RUNBOOK.md, README.md, docs/specs/UX_log_system.md.
What Jessy should use: After powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\Create-Agent-UI-Shortcut.ps1, use the new Mimi AI Agent UI.lnk on Desktop or Start Menu → Programs → ai-agent (target pythonw.exe). Pin that .lnk to the taskbar.
Old pins: Yes — unpin any taskbar icon that was tied to the old shortcut (or to .cmd), then pin the newly generated Mimi AI Agent UI.lnk (or drag the new .lnk to the taskbar). Otherwise Windows keeps launching the old target.
Validation here: This environment cannot click Jessy’s real icon or see Windows thumbnails. On her machine: run the create script once → double-click the new .lnk** → confirm **no** new PowerShell/cmd window and **browser** opens → use Agent as usual. **Debug check:** Launch-Agent-UI.cmd` still shows a console.
UI-09B? Maybe: if anything still looks like a terminal after re-pinning, next suspects are Streamlit child processes, Windows Terminal default host, or a non-repo shortcut — worth a short follow-up only if the issue survives the pythonw + direct .lnk path.
Stopping after UI-09A as requested.
